tweets.text:
More Americans are stuck without work for longer:

The number of Americans unemployed for 27+ weeks rose by +155,000 in May to 1.99 million, the highest since December 2021.

YoY, this figure has surged +524,000, the largest annual increase since August 2021.

As a % of unemployment, this metric is up to 27.5%, the highest since December 2021.

This percentage has risen +9.0 points since the start of 2023 and is now above every post-recession peak, excluding 2008 and 2020.

An increasing number of Americans are struggling to find employment.

tweets.text:
Demand for protection against a South Korean stock market decline is surging:

Put option open interest in the South Korea ETF, $EWY, is up to ~880,000 contracts, the highest in at least 12 months.

The total number of outstanding put option contracts has QUADRUPLED over the last several weeks.

By comparison, call option open interest has increased +80,000 over the same period, to ~400,000 contracts, near the highest in a year.

To put this into perspective, both put and call option open interest stood at ~50,000 contracts in January 2026.

Meanwhile, $EWY declined -14% on Friday, posting its largest daily drop since the 2020 pandemic crash.

Investors are rushing to hedge against a correction in South Korea.

tweets.text:
On this day in 2000, Judge Thomas Penfield Jackson ordered Microsoft broken into two companies: one for Windows, one for everything else. The ruling came after Microsoft was found guilty of violating the Sherman Antitrust Act by bundling Internet Explorer with Windows to crush Netscape.

Microsoft's stock had already dropped 43% from its December 1999 peak by the time the breakup order came down. The company's market cap went from $619 billion to roughly $350 billion in six months. Bill Gates called it "the most massive government overreach in the technology industry."

The breakup never happened. An appeals court overturned Jackson's order in 2001, partly because he'd made public comments comparing Microsoft to drug dealers. Microsoft settled with the DOJ in 2002 and agreed to share its programming interfaces with competitors. The stock didn't recover its 1999 highs for another 16 years.
tweets.full_text:
On this day in 2000, Judge Thomas Penfield Jackson ordered Microsoft broken into two companies: one for Windows, one for everything else. The ruling came after Microsoft was found guilty of violating the Sherman Antitrust Act by bundling Internet Explorer with Windows to crush Netscape.

Microsoft's stock had already dropped 43% from its December 1999 peak by the time the breakup order came down. The company's market cap went from $619 billion to roughly $350 billion in six months. Bill Gates called it "the most massive government overreach in the technology industry."

The breakup never happened. An appeals court overturned Jackson's order in 2001, partly because he'd made public comments comparing Microsoft to drug dealers. Microsoft settled with the DOJ in 2002 and agreed to share its programming interfaces with competitors. The stock didn't recover its 1999 highs for another 16 years.

tweets.text:
Here's my random stat for the weekend as everyone wonders what happens next.

Since 1995, there have been six calendar years where the S&P 500 grew earnings by >20% (95, 03, 04, 10, 18, and 21).

All but 2018 ended w/ double digit returns. Of course, the Fed was hiking in 18. 

2026 EPS tracking for 23ish% growth.

If the Fed actually hikes*, the 2018 comp sticks out (early year correction/late-year mini bear).

*I don't expect the Fed to hike just remove easing bias.

thread_tweets.text:
Why not higher despite SOH closure? The short answer is that China went on a buyer's strike. Imports down ~4 MMBpd, which means that the deficit we are dealing w/ is closer to a 3-4 MMBpd. It's meaningful but not the emergency situation of a 8-10 MMBpd deficit.

Those prediction markets are not a good gauge of consensus. I don't know how that Kalshi contract is calculated but my recollection is that they require traffic to hit a certain pre-crisis percentage (60%). There's a good chance that SOH traffic will never hit that level even under best case scenario.

In this environment, predictions hinge on two variables: When does the SOH "open" and when does China come back to the market? China has plenty of capacity to wait.

Those prediction markets are not a good gauge of consensus. There's a good chance that SOH traffic will never hit that level even under best case scenario.

In this environment, predictions hinge on two variables: When does the SOH "open" and when does China come back to the market? China has plenty of capacity to wait.

tweets.text:
I see this chart everywhere but have no idea where he is getting the blue line value.

In Bbg, you can look up LT analyst estimates for the S&P 500. It is a sparsely populated field that is currently around 16%.

If you look at estimates per calendar year, 27-29 imply ~12% EPS growth. The only forward year tracking >20% is 2026, which is half over (i.e. in the bag).
